title: rosa × collina subsp. saxatilis
intro: Rosa × collina subsp. saxatilis, also known as the wild rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa × collina hybrid, a cross between the Rosa canina and Rosa majalis species. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa × collina subsp. saxatilis include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce stress, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Rosa × collina subsp. saxatilis is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
